summary.verdict: PASS. Primary metric (green after one revision) = 14/20 = 70%, meeting the >=70% final-green band; par-of-final-green = 14/14 = 100%, meeting the >=80% band; both are well clear of the <50% FAIL floor. This is the first round with a diagnostic-feedback cycle, and it clears the preregistered bar on the metric it was designed to move. First-shot (secondary) rose to 40% from the round-3 clean baseline of 26.3%.
summary.first_shot_rate: 40% (8/20 first-shot green), up from the round-3 clean baseline of 26.3%. Tracked for comparison only; not the PASS/FAIL metric.
summary.final_rate: 70% final-green (14/20) after one revision; par-of-final-green 100% (14/14). PASS on both preregistered conditions.
delta_analysis: Cycle value: the diagnostic-feedback loop converted 6 of 12 first-shot failures to green (a3, b3, c2, e1, e3, e4), lifting the rate from 40% to 70% (+30pp). This is the headline: one revision round roughly halved the failure population.

Par quality is uniformly high: every final-green solution was par (14/14), and every surviving failure is a checker-REJECT (worst_final = API_MISUSE), not a perf/par miss. The residual problem is legality, not optimization quality.

CURED families (revision fixed them): whatever defects the 6 recovered tasks carried were surfaced and correctable from the feedback text alone — the cycle works when the defect is a locally-fixable spelling/derivation error the diagnostic can point at.

SURVIVING family 1 — in-loop region naming (OWN-11): survived in BOTH d1 and d4. A borrow_expr inside loop @l naming a region minted OUTSIDE the loop body. Recurrence across two tasks + survival through diagnostics is the strongest doc/design signal in the round. Responsible section: OWN-11 (C1 card) and its worked example.

SURVIVING family 2 — construct/loop grammar in atom position and missing labels (GRAM). a4: nullary construct AlreadyRegistered() placed in a fieldinit/atom slot (GRAM-5/8/9) instead of being let-bound. d4: bare `loop {}`/`break;` omitting the mandatory LABEL (GRAM-4). Responsible sections: GRAM-4, GRAM-9, and the C1 hnull let-bind pattern.

SURVIVING family 3 — effect-row derivation through deref (EFF-2). c1: both conformers omit reads('v) despite reading env/value fields via deref; the writer adopted a wrong rule ('reading through a &uniq borrow adds no reads clause') that contradicts the blessed count_visit conformer. Responsible section: EFF-2 + the conformer catalog prose for count_visit / keep_big.

SURVIVING family 4 — type/const-argument explicitness on generic scalar ops (a2). `ieq(n, 0_u64)` omits the required `<u64>`; the writer conflated 'dotless' (no .mode suffix) with 'no type argument'. CAT-1a receiver-fixed suppression is table-ops-only and does not cover receiver-less scalar compares, which TYPE-5/FN-2 require explicit. Responsible section: CAT-1a suppression scope vs TYPE-5/FN-2.

SURVIVING family 5 — reborrowing / borrow model (e2). Reborrowing a field (&uniq 'e deref(h).items) out of a borrowed wrapper struct, which v0 lacks (no reborrowing, T-A). Critically, the diagnostic cycle NEVER surfaced this — the three applied fixes were each individually correct; the real defect predates and survived the cycle undetected. This is a diagnostic blind spot, not just a doc gap. Responsible section: C1 card (T-A, no reborrowing; helpers written over the parts).

SURVIVING family 6 — genuinely undocumented spellings (spec gaps, not writer error): cq_new arity/binder site `cq_new<Record, spsc, 10>()` vs the only documented `cq_new<u64>()` (d1, and the seq/queue choice space in e2). Both were honestly flagged by the writer; these are true underspecification, so the fix is documentation, not writer correction. Responsible section: S.3 red box (cq_new instantiation).

Interpretation: cured defects were feedback-actionable local errors; surviving defects cluster into (a) recurring rule blind spots the writer re-violates even after a hint — OWN-11 above all — and (b) doc/spec gaps the diagnostic cannot resolve because the correct spelling isn't attested. Family recurrence (OWN-11 twice) and the e2 blind spot are the actionable signals.
remaining_defects: a2 (REJECT): `ieq(n, 0_u64)` missing explicit `<u64>` type arg on a receiver-less generic scalar compare; CAT-1a suppression is table-ops-only, TYPE-5/FN-2 require explicit args. Legal: `ieq<u64>(n, 0_u64)`. (Plus a cosmetic effect-row rationale slip on seq_remove_at's `traps`/region-confinement — code correct.)
a4 (REJECT): nullary construct `AlreadyRegistered()` in an atom/fieldinit position (`Err(error: AlreadyRegistered())`); GRAM-9 hard error. Must let-bind first (C1 hnull pattern).

c1 (REJECT): both conformers omit reads('v) for deref field reads (deref(env).count, deref(env).cutoff, deref(value).expires_at); EFF-2 undeclared-but-exhibited, both-directions error. Wrong rule adopted ('reading through &uniq adds no reads'), contradicts count_visit.

d1 (REJECT): OWN-11 — in-loop `&uniq 'e r`/`&uniq 'e dst` name region 'e minted outside the loop; must mint an inner `region 'f {}`. Plus unattested `cq_new<Record, spsc, 10>()` arity (honestly flagged spec gap).

d4 (REJECT): OWN-11 — in-loop `cq_try_send(&uniq 'e t, ...)` names outer region 'e; must mint fresh in-loop region. Plus GRAM-4 — bare `loop {}`/`break;` missing mandatory LABEL. Plus task-compliance: red-box rule cited not quoted verbatim.

e2 (REJECT, non-par): reborrows a field out of a borrowed wrapper struct (&uniq 'e deref(h).items from &uniq 'e History), unspellable in v0 (T-A, no reborrowing) — undetected by the diagnostic cycle. Plus WRONG_CHOICE: seq_remove_at(s, 0_u64) front-removal is O(len) per eviction (SEQ-4), the anti-pattern C2 supersedes; defensible-but-not-blessed for the affine-Event case. This is the only non-par failure (final_par=false).
next_fixes: OWN-11 (C1 card): top priority — it recurred (d1, d4) and survived diagnostics. Add a prominent, repeated worked example showing the in-loop fresh-region mint (`region 'f {}` inside the loop) as the ONLY legal way to borrow a receiver inside `loop @l`, and explicitly call out the outer-region-minted-around-loop shape as the canonical rejection.

CAT-1a / TYPE-5 / FN-2 (a2): state unambiguously that receiver-fixed type-arg suppression is table-ops-only, and that receiver-less generic scalar ops (ieq/ilt/... over T) ALWAYS need explicit `<T>`. Add a 'dotless is not typeless' note to kill the 'no .mode suffix means no type arg' conflation.

EFF-2 + conformer catalog (c1): make explicit that reading an env/value field through deref exhibits reads('v) even through a &uniq borrow; annotate count_visit's reads('v) as 'from one deref field read' and state the keep_big pattern drops writes/traps from the ceiling but NEVER its reads clause. Directly refute 'reading through &uniq adds no reads.'

GRAM-9 / GRAM-5 (a4) and GRAM-4 (d4): reinforce that a construct (even nullary) may not sit in an atom/fieldinit slot — must be let-bound — and that loop/break LABELs are mandatory, not optional. Cross-link the C1 hnull and `loop @scan` examples.

C1 reborrowing / T-A (e2): add an explicit no-reborrowing example — helpers must be written over the field parts (pass &uniq 'e seq<Event,100> minted by the owner), never over a borrowed wrapper struct; contrast the ring_push place-op pattern that avoids minting a field borrow.

Document the cq_new full instantiation/binder spelling in the S.3 red box (d1) so the tag/const-arg form (ep/K binder) is attested rather than guessed; likewise clarify the blessed affine FIFO route so e2's seq-vs-C2 choice is unambiguous.

Close the diagnostic blind spot: the e2 reborrow defect was never surfaced by the cycle. Extend the feedback generator to flag reborrow-out-of-borrowed-struct and WRONG_CHOICE (perf anti-pattern) classes, not just locally-fixable spelling/derivation errors.
caveats: Grader-not-compiler: 'green' is adjudicated by a grader, not the production xlc checker. Rejections and par verdicts are the grader's best judgment; a real checker could disagree at the margin in either direction.

Diagnostic quality is almost certainly better than a real compiler's would be — the feedback is expert-written and targets the actual defect. So the 70% final-green (and the +30pp cure delta) is plausibly an UPPER bound on what a real compiler's diagnostics would deliver; discount accordingly when projecting to shipped tooling.

Diagnostics also have a demonstrated blind spot: e2's real defect (reborrow, T-A) predated and survived the cycle uncaught while three correct-but-irrelevant fixes were applied. High cure rate does not mean diagnostics see everything.
Small n=20 with wide CIs; single round. 70% sits exactly on the PASS threshold — a one- or two-task swing crosses it, so treat the PASS as provisional, not comfortable.

First-shot 40% vs round-3 26.3% is a favorable but not strictly apples-to-apples comparison (round 3 was a 'clean baseline' without the feedback cycle); read it as directional, not a controlled delta.

All 6 surviving failures are checker-REJECTs (API_MISUSE), and e2 is the sole non-par (14/14 par among greens). The residual risk is legality/writability, not optimization quality — the perf/par story is clean.
Codenames and rule IDs (OWN-11, EFF-2, CAT-1a, etc.) are repo-log internal; in any owner-facing plain-language summary, describe these as 'the loop-borrow rule', 'the effect-tracking rule', etc.
